blocks.text: Jose Rizal Memorial State University commits to provide quality

instruction to all its students delivered by qualified and competent faculty members. With the influx of students in the University due to the free higher education, the student population has increased significantly. Hence, this impacts the faculty workload and eventually requires additional teaching force for the various program offerings across campuses.

Having operated for more than ten (10) years as a university, it has encountered a number of challenges which have been recurring and unresolved since the existing policies on faculty workload are limited in nature.

Hence, the university needs to update and align its institutional policies especially on the regular teaching load of permanent faculty members including the payment of honorarium and overtime pay for the teaching overload pursuant
to the 2024 Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) issued by the Commission on Audit.
Section 1 In accordance with the Article 83 of the Labor Code of the
Philippines, as amended, the normal hours of work of school personnel shall not exceed eight (8) hours a day.
Any work done in addition to the eight (8) hours daily work shall constitute overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).
Section 2 Each head of the department in the SUC may allow

flexible working hours for his/her faculty members so they can perform their four-fold functions of instruction, consultation, research and extension services, provided that the prescribed forty hours of work per week shall
strictly be implemented (CSC MC No. 19, s.2005).
Section 3 Agencies in government may adopt compressed

workweek that refers to a work arrangement whereby the forty (40) hours workweek for five (5) days of the government officials or employees is compressed to four (4) days or less, as may be applicable (CSC MC No. 06, s.2022).
Section 4 The regular teaching load of full-time academic teaching
personnel shall be determined by the higher education
